key_insight: A physics-informed CNN-LSTM can forecast long-term photoluminescence degradation of unseen perovskite samples from accelerated ageing data, while remaining interpretable.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster stability screening of candidate semiconductor materials, reducing experimental turnaround in materials discovery and reliability testing.
method: Collected 167 metal-halide perovskite samples spanning different compositions and crystallinities.
Stressed samples with heat and illumination while recording >86k photoluminescence spectra.
Featurized spectra with a physics-informed model and trained a hybrid CNN-LSTM to predict future PL intensity.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on unseen samples, generalized across material groups, and outperformed baseline benchmarks in forecasting degradation trajectories.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the physics-informed features and the training dataset, so transfer to new material systems or stress conditions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: Physics-informed time-series forecasting of perovskite photoluminescence stability
